commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=cca7b9cb549b0f792f9f907361a89840ad3a8c5a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds optional AbortSignal propagation from QMD memory search into direct qmd subprocess execution and adds regression tests for aborted qmd search children.

PR surface: Source +79, Tests +128. Total +207 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main passes a memory_search deadline signal into manager.search, and source inspection shows the QMD grouped-search helper still runs qmd subprocesses without any caller signal.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can thread the existing signal through the grouped QMD search helper and add one regression test without choosing a new product direction.

target.title: fix(memory): abort orphaned qmd search subprocess when memory_search times out
target.body:
## Summary

PR #91742 ("abort orphaned embedding work when memory_search times out") added an `AbortSignal` to `MemorySearchManager.search()` so that when the 15s `memory_search` tool deadline fires, in-flight embedding work is cancelled instead of running orphaned. That fix wired the signal through the **builtin** manager (`manager.ts` consumes `opts.signal` for `embedQueryWithRetry` and the `signal.aborted` early-out), but the **QMD** manager — the other backend behind the exact same `MemorySearchManager.search` interface — silently ignored it.

With the QMD backend, `memory_search` returns "timed out after 15s" to the agent after 15s, but the spawned `qmd query`/`qmd search` subprocess keeps running for the full QMD command timeout (`memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs`, whose embed-heavy default was raised to 600s in PR #87572). So the orphaned-work leak that #91742 fixed for the builtin backend was still present for QMD users: the agent has already moved on, but a `qmd` process keeps churning embeddings/vector search for up to ~10 minutes.

This is the same symmetry the original fix intended; it just missed the second backend and the subprocess cancellation boundary in `runCliCommand`.

## What changed

- `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.ts`: `runCliCommand` now accepts an optional `signal: AbortSignal`. When the signal aborts (or is already aborted on entry), the spawned child is `SIGKILL`ed immediately and the call rejects with the abort reason (preserving the caller's deadline message). Resolution/rejection is funneled through a single `settle()` guard so abort, timeout, `error`, and `close` cannot double-settle.
- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts`: `QmdMemoryManager.search()` accepts `opts.signal` (matching the builtin manager and the `MemorySearchManager` interface added in #91742). It fast-fails if the signal is already aborted, and threads the signal through `runQmdSearch` → `runQmd` → `runCliCommand` for the default direct-`qmd` subprocess path (including the `query` fallback). When the caller's deadline fires, the qmd subprocess is killed instead of left orphaned.

Scope is intentionally focused on the default direct-qmd subprocess path plus a universal pre-flight abort guard (which also short-circuits the optional mcporter bridge before it spawns anything). This mirrors #91742's targeted approach rather than threading a signal through every optional code path.

## Real behavior proof (real runtime, outside tests)

Ran the **real exported `runCliCommand`** via `node --import tsx` against a real OS subprocess, proving abort kills the actual process (cross-subprocess boundary) instead of waiting out the command timeout:

```
$ TSX_TSCONFIG_PATH=tsconfig.json node --import tsx /tmp/abort-proof.mts
[A] normal completion stdout: "hello-from-child"
[B] child OS pid: 541090 alive before abort: true
[B] runCliCommand rejected after 7 ms with: "memory_search timed out after 15s"
[B] child pid 541090 alive AFTER abort: false

RESULT: PASS - abort killed the real subprocess and rejected promptly (no orphan).
```

The proof script spawns a child that records its own PID and `sleep 30`s, with `timeoutMs: 600_000` (mimicking the qmd embed timeout). It confirms via `process.kill(pid, 0)` that the real OS process is **alive before abort** and **dead after abort**, that `runCliCommand` rejects in **7ms** (not 600s) with the caller's deadline message, and that a no-signal short command still completes normally (Case A).

## Tests and validation

- `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts` (+2 tests): `runCliCommand` kills the child with `SIGKILL` and rejects with the abort reason when the signal aborts mid-run; and rejects immediately without spawning when the signal is already aborted. 16/16 pass.
- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts` (+2 tests): `QmdMemoryManager.search({ signal })` kills the in-flight `qmd query` subprocess (asserted via the child `kill("SIGKILL")` spy) when the caller aborts, and rejects before spawning when the signal is already aborted. 131/131 pass.
- **Negative control**: reverting the qmd-manager wiring (dropping `signal` propagation + the pre-flight guard) makes both new qmd-manager tests fail — the `search()` promise never settles after abort because the never-closing subprocess is left orphaned, exactly the leak being fixed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json` and the memory-host-sdk typecheck pass.

## Risk checklist

- Behavior is unchanged when no `signal` is passed (all parameters/opts fields are optional; existing callers are unaffected).
- `settle()` guard prevents double resolve/reject across abort/timeout/error/close; the abort listener is removed on settle to avoid leaks.
- The abort reason prefers the caller-supplied error so the existing "memory_search timed out after 15s" message is preserved end to end.
- No public API removed; only optional `signal` fields added. No config or schema changes.
target.author: alix-007

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
